title: Rollinia microcarpa

intro: Rollinia microcarpa, also known as the 'Wild Soursop', is a species of plant in the Annonaceae family, native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rollinia microcarpa include annonaceous acetogenins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
